frameworks.title: Shareholder oppression or exclusion
elements: A shareholder or member right is identified
Conduct is prejudicial, unfairly discriminatory, or exclusionary
The conduct is anchored to dated records, company documents, or communications
defences: The complained-of act was authorized by the company constitution or law
The complaining party lacked the asserted right
The record does not anchor prejudice to the respondent's conduct
remedies: Court-supervised relief restoring rights or access
Damages or a buy-out where the forum permits it

frameworks.title: Unauthorized account access or digital interference
elements: The system, account, device, or credential is identified
Unauthorized access, possession, interference, or misuse is grounded in sealed findings
The conduct is linked to a forum-specific offence or complaint pathway
defences: No reliable attribution to the accused actor
The record shows suspicion only, not anchored access conduct
remedies: Preservation of logs, devices, and access records
Interdict or protective procedural steps where available

frameworks.title: Disputed execution of operative documents
elements: The document and its legal function are identified
The record anchors a material conflict about signature, authorization, or operative status
The conflict affects rights, obligations, or filing posture
defences: A valid countersignature or ratification cured the defect
The disputed document was not legally operative
The contradiction is rhetorical rather than factual
remedies: Complaint framing that separates proof issues from liability conclusions
